headline: RN continues to enrich far-right allies through EU Parliament funds
description: Mathieu Balavoine, a figure in the French radical far right convicted of violence, has received payments from the Rassemblement National's group at the European Parliament for his services. The party is under judicial scrutiny.

headline: Monaco explosion: The Ukrainian-born oligarch's shady business dealings
Europe tries to strengthen defenses against Chinese competition
France will likely face third heatwave of the year from this weekend
description: Temperatures are set to drop tomorrow and Thursday across the country, before rising again Friday in the southern half. The duration, intensity and geographical extent of the episode remain uncertain at this stage.

headline: Tax hikes and pension reform: OECD lays out plan to restore France's public finances
description: Without decisive action, France's public debt could soar to 127% of GDP by 2030, the international organization warns. It recommends a mix of tax hikes, an increased retirement age, and spending cuts to tackle the issue.

headline: Nearly 100 players born in France were selected for the 2026 World Cup – but only 23 play for France
description: It's a testament to France's success as a breeding ground for international football talent. And sometimes, France misses out on promising dual-nationality players, like Morocco's Ayyoub Bouaddi.

headline: 250 years of American independence: In 1976, a bicentennial celebrated with patriotism despite divisions
description: 'America 250' (10/13). In 1976, the United States turned a page on one of the most turbulent chapters in its history. After the Vietnam War and the resignation of Richard Nixon, the bicentennial offered Americans a moment of relief. Minority rights, social justice – new debates continued to stir society, notably led by the economist Jeremy Rifkin and the 'red, white and blue' left.

The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
description: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.

headline: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
description: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.

headline: In the DRC's Ebola epicenter, a 'cascade of deaths' and fear of contamination
description: Le Monde traveled to Mongbwalu, a town in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, where the Ebola virus outbreak began. As the death toll continues to rise, local health facilities and NGOs have become overwhelmed, hampered by a shortage of resources.
byline: Morgane Le Cam and  Philémon Barbier
